title: Stratified scheduling experiments
body:
This PR is not meant to be merged. I'm opening this to get feedback/continue discussion from #2446 

For now this only adds a visitor that aims to assign each node in an Ast tree to a region (Active, Inactive, NBA, etc.) using new `m_regionId` field.

General idea behind that is to split functions into Active and Reactive groups using this information and then to modify the generated code so that we first evaluate all functions belonging to the Active region and then we take care of the Reactive region.

Some tests with `--output-split-cfuncs 1` show that expressions inside functions remain correctly tagged all the way to the final Ast tree (`region` values: `-1` - uninitialized, `0` - Active, `4` - Reactive) and each function contains expressions from a single region.
```
...
    1:2: CFUNC 0x563efeba3120 region: -1  <e1846> {c47ab}  _initial__TOP__4 [SLOW] [STATICU]
    1:2:3: COMMENT 0x563efeb9d2a0 region: -1  <e1660> {c47ab}  INITIAL
    1:2:3: ASSIGN 0x563efeb90f30 region: 4  <e1924> {c48ah} @dt=0x563efeb95160@(G/wu32/1)
    1:2:3:1: CONST 0x563efeb91000 region: 4  <e1922> {c48aj} @dt=0x563efeb95160@(G/wu32/1)  1'h0
    1:2:3:2: VARREF 0x563efeb91170 region: 4  <e1923> {c48af} @dt=0x563efeb95160@(G/wu32/1)  c [LV] => VAR 0x563efeb7cb50 region: 0  <e704> {c1cs} @dt=0x563efeb671a0@(G/w1)  c [PO] OUTPUT [P] [VSTATIC]  WIRE
    1:2:3: DISPLAY 0x563efeb912a0 region: 4  <e275> {c49af}
    1:2:3:1: SFORMATF 0x563efeb91370 region: 4  <e274> {c49af} @dt=0x563efeb69620@(G/str)  PRG1
    1:2:3: ASSIGN 0x563efeb91460 region: 4  <e1927> {c50ah} @dt=0x563efeb95160@(G/wu32/1)
    1:2:3:1: CONST 0x563efeb91530 region: 4  <e1925> {c50aj} @dt=0x563efeb95160@(G/wu32/1)  1'h1
    1:2:3:2: VARREF 0x563efeb916a0 region: 4  <e1926> {c50af} @dt=0x563efeb95160@(G/wu32/1)  c [LV] => VAR 0x563efeb7cb50 region: 0  <e704> {c1cs} @dt=0x563efeb671a0@(G/w1)  c [PO] OUTPUT [P] [VSTATIC]  WIRE
    1:2: CFUNC 0x563efeba3530 region: -1  <e1848> {c25ab}  _initial__TOP__5 [SLOW] [STATICU]
    1:2:3: COMMENT 0x563efeb9d390 region: -1  <e1672> {c25ab}  INITIAL
    1:2:3: ASSIGN 0x563efeb922c0 region: 0  <e1930> {c26ah} @dt=0x563efeb95160@(G/wu32/1)
    1:2:3:1: CONST 0x563efeb92390 region: 0  <e1928> {c26aj} @dt=0x563efeb95160@(G/wu32/1)  1'h1
    1:2:3:2: VARREF 0x563efeb92500 region: 0  <e1929> {c26af} @dt=0x563efeb95160@(G/wu32/1)  d [LV] => VAR 0x563efeb7cf10 region: 0  <e710> {c1dh} @dt=0x563efeb671a0@(G/w1)  d [PO] OUTPUT [P] [VSTATIC]  WIRE
    1:2:3: DISPLAY 0x563efeb92630 region: 0  <e154> {c27af}
    1:2:3:1: SFORMATF 0x563efeb92700 region: 0  <e153> {c27af} @dt=0x563efeb69620@(G/str)  MOD0
    1:2:3: ASSIGN 0x563efeb927f0 region: 0  <e1933> {c28ah} @dt=0x563efeb95160@(G/wu32/1)
    1:2:3:1: CONST 0x563efeb928c0 region: 0  <e1931> {c28aj} @dt=0x563efeb95160@(G/wu32/1)  1'h0
    1:2:3:2: VARREF 0x563efeb92a30 region: 0  <e1932> {c28af} @dt=0x563efeb95160@(G/wu32/1)  d [LV] => VAR 0x563efeb7cf10 region: 0  <e710> {c1dh} @dt=0x563efeb671a0@(G/w1)  d [PO] OUTPUT [P] [VSTATIC]  WIRE
...
```
My aim for now is to look through visitors responsible for generating functions find a proper place to assign each function to a region.
Once I have this data I will try to group the functions so that ones from Active region are executed first.

Any feedback on general approach or code is welcome
